differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune limbic encephalitis (e.g., anti-LGI1 or anti-GABA-B)
Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
Neurodegenerative disease (e.g., Alzheimer's disease) with superimposed inflammatory change
next_steps: Send serum and CSF for a comprehensive autoimmune encephalitis and paraneoplastic antibody panel
Perform CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast to screen for underlying malignancy
Obtain CSF cytology and flow cytometry to rule out leptomeningeal spread of malignancy or lymphoma
Initiate antiseizure therapy with lacosamide and monitor for seizure activity
Consult neurology for potential trial of high-dose corticosteroids if infection and malignancy are excluded, as this is therapeutic for suspected CAA-ri and autoimmune encephalitis
Consider brain biopsy if clinical, imaging, and laboratory workup remain inconclusive
